comments.text: You may want to add the related sequence A005235 (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
This would imply every primorial is the difference of two primes. I dont know any proof of this weaker claim. The left-most inequality can be omitted.

But similar to the twin prime conjecture, every primorial should be the difference of infinitely many different pairs of two primes (by Schinzel's hypothesis H) But proving one example and proving infinitely many examples in general, are likely both hard here
This conjecture is similar to Fortune's conjecture, and similarly intractable with current methods.
